trivia_bullets.text: Printed in Core Set 2021 (M21), released July 3, 2020. Sits in M21's black-white lifegain limited archetype, where gaining 3 or more life in a single turn was the recurring payoff condition across multiple black cards. Has not been reprinted as of May 2026.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Core Set 2021 Limited Preview (2020)

trivia_bullets.text: "Drink from the vein? How provincial." —Lord Duchorvin [Scryfall]. Lord Duchorvin does not appear elsewhere in printed MTG canon as a named character with lore pages — the name is flavor-only. The line positions a vampire aristocrat sneering at primitive direct feeding, asserting social hierarchy through method of consumption.

The cost-reduction mechanic (costs {3} less if you gained 3+ life this turn) makes this a free recursive effect in a functioning lifegain deck. In M21's B/W limited archetype, it combined with Silversmote Ghoul: the same lifegain threshold enables Silversmote's graveyard return AND Sanguine Indulgence's full cost discount.
sources: MTGArena Zone: Sanguine Indulgence card page

trivia_bullets.text: Legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander at release. EDHREC rank 14,899 reflects low Commander adoption — at common rarity the effect competes poorly with tutor-accessible recursion in higher-powered lists.

related_cards.name: Silversmote Ghoul
related_cards.note: Shares the lifegain-3 threshold trigger; forms the core recursion engine of the M21 B/W lifegain limited archetype

related_cards.name: Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose
related_cards.note: M21 vampire mythic whose drain-on-lifegain rewards the same lifegain-dense shell Sanguine Indulgence inhabits

related_cards.name: Griffin Aerie
related_cards.note: White lifegain payoff the Hipsters M21 preview identified as a pillar of the B/W archetype alongside these black lifegain cards
